title: sarracenia × mariannae
intro: Sarracenia × mariannae, also known as the Marietta Sundew, is a naturally occurring hybrid plant species between Sarracenia alata and Sarracenia psittacina. This carnivorous plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The Marietta Sundew is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its unique compounds. In herbal preparations, Sarracenia × mariannae is often used in tea or tincture form to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
